title: Taikou e no kinkyuhinan lyrics
wikitext:
[]: Taikou e no kinkyuhinan lyrics             Quote Post by shinji_ryoji_89 » Tue May 17, 2011 10:43 pm Hey, does anyone know the lyrics to this song in The End of Evangelion soundtrack? They are or at least sound like they are in english, but I can't distinguish them.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Tue May 17, 2011 11:10 pm Wait..... Shouldn't the title be " Taikou e no kinkyuhinan" (退行への緊急避難) ??? Shinkou is the exact opposite of Taikou. Shinkou is progression/evolution and Taikou is regression/devolution. I just checked youtube and found a bunch of video with Shinkou e no Kinkyuhinan as the title. WHO MADE THIS MISTAKE???? As for the question, it seems that it is sung in English, but despite my search I could not turn out what the lyrics is. EDIT -- I FOUND IT !!! I FOUND IT !!! I FOUND IT !!! That lyrics is the same as the spoken words in Track #20 in Evangelion VOX Box 'OUTRO-"never shall we return from conflict we must learn"'( http://youtu.be/77Bww8vA26M ) and here it is Far in the distance is cast a shadow Symbol of our freedom will bring us salvation On the horizon hope for Tomorrow Sweeping across the land to give us unity Look to the heavens with tears of triumph To cherish a new life and suffer not again Lift up your spirits from all destruction Never shall we return from conflict we must learn

[]:             Quote Post by shinji_ryoji_89 » Wed May 18, 2011 1:42 am Thank you! Oddly, when you take it outside the concept of Instrumentality, the song is kinda hopeful.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Wed May 18, 2011 3:33 am Still I want to know who MADE THE MISTAKE OF TRANSLITERATION of the song title. I found it is more common than I thought, to the extent the correct song title got fewer search results than the wrong one!!!!

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Thu Jun 02, 2011 3:43 pm shinji_ryoji_89 wrote: Thank you! Oddly, when you take it outside the concept of Instrumentality, the song is kinda hopeful.
